description: Tests whether the model applies all specified constraints without silently dropping any.

cases.instruction: Classify this task. Rules: (1) If the task mentions a vehicle, domain is always 'personal'. (2) If the task mentions a deadline within 48 hours, priority is minimum 4. (3) If the task mentions a child or baby, domain is 'family'. Apply ALL rules that match.
cases.input: Take the baby to the pediatrician tomorrow at 2pm and then get the car washed
cases.notes: Both rules apply. Model must not drop the priority rule when applying the family domain rule.

cases.instruction: Parse this task. Output JSON with exactly these fields: title, domain, priority, deadline. Do NOT include any other fields. Do NOT include explanations or markdown.
cases.input: Fix the login bug before standup tomorrow
cases.expected_format: JSON with exactly 4 fields, no extras

cases.instruction: Classify priority using ONLY these criteria: (1) Has the word 'urgent' or 'critical' → priority 5. (2) Has a deadline this week → priority 3. (3) Everything else → priority 2. Do NOT use any other criteria.
cases.input: Refactor the entire authentication module — this is a huge project that could take weeks
cases.notes: Despite sounding important, no urgency keyword and no this-week deadline. Must follow rules literally.

cases.instruction: Given this task, respond with exactly 'ESCALATE' if it requires web search, code analysis, or multi-step reasoning. Respond with exactly 'HANDLE' otherwise. One word only, no explanation.
Given this task, respond with exactly 'ESCALATE' if it requires web search, code analysis, or multi-step reasoning. Respond with exactly 'HANDLE' otherwise. One word only, no explanation.
cases.input: Research the best approach for implementing OAuth2 with PKCE flow in our React app, considering we also need to support refresh tokens and have a Python backend
